threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think buying a hot drink means stepping into a café, not queuing at a stall with thirty people, right?
Last night on the 59, a guy was singing to a half-empty bus like he was headlining Glastonbury. You could tell he was on his way back from a wild night.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a toddler in a pink tutu chasing a pigeon while their dad brewed coffee at the park. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know that feeling when you’re on the U-Bahn and suddenly it stops for no reason? Just another day in Kreuzberg, where the trains seem to think it's on vacation.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour due to random outages. Tourists just look confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo playing a saxophone on the bus while the driver was stuck in traffic. Only in SF can you get a live concert with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just quirky bars and plazas, but they miss the quiet morning rituals at the local bakeries where we share the day's gossip over coffee.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by how they still take photos of the vermut at 12:30 on a Sunday. Locals know it's all about the company, not the Instagram shot.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You order a flat white and look baffled when it comes in a tiny cup, like you were expecting a venti-sized mug. Classic rookie mistake.
Caught the N73 last night and the driver was belting out old school garage tunes. Made the 3am journey feel like a party, even with all the drunks.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted a wild rooster strutting down Richardstraße at 8 AM, like it owned the place. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a bar hidden behind a kebab shop, where the cocktails are named after protest slogans. It's a wild mix of culture and nightlife!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local if they can name the taco truck that’s always parked on 24th and Mission, but can’t find Civic Center on a map.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 today at Civic Center, and the driver had a full on debate with a pigeon at the stop. It won, obviously. Classic SF moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just Gaudí, but really it's the hum of locals at the plaça and the chaos of the festa major that define us.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the xurros stand, trying to figure out if churros come with chocolate or just sugar.
